questions.text: Where is the Guantanamo Bay prison located?
questions.explanation: Guantanamo Bay is a US naval base in Cuba where prisoners were kept illegally.

questions.text: Which organization reported that prisoners in Guantanamo Bay were being tortured?
questions.explanation: Amnesty International is an international human rights organization.

questions.text: The ethnic massacre in Kosovo was carried out by the government of ________.
questions.explanation: The Serbian government under Milosevic targeted ethnic Albanians in Kosovo.

questions.text: Rights are reasonable claims of persons recognized by society and ________.
questions.explanation: For a claim to become a right, it must be recognized by the state as a legal entitlement.

questions.text: Rights given to citizens of India by the Constitution are called ________ Rights.
questions.explanation: There are six categories of Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution.

questions.text: Which right ensures that all citizens are equal before the law?
questions.explanation: The Right to Equality prevents discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth.

questions.text: The practice of 'Untouchability' was abolished by which right?
questions.explanation: Article 17 of the Constitution makes the practice of untouchability a punishable offense.

questions.text: Which right allows citizens to form associations or unions?
questions.explanation: Freedom of assembly and association is part of the Right to Freedom.

questions.text: A person cannot be detained in custody for more than ________ without being produced before a magistrate.
questions.explanation: This is a protection against illegal arrest and detention.

questions.text: Which right prohibits 'begar' or forced labor?
questions.explanation: Begar is a practice where workers are forced to work for free or low wages.

questions.text: According to the Right against Exploitation, children below the age of ________ cannot work in hazardous jobs.
questions.explanation: Child labor in factories, mines or other dangerous occupations is banned.

questions.text: The Right to Freedom of Religion means that:
options: Everyone must follow the government's religion
Everyone has the right to profess, practice and propagate any religion
questions.explanation: India is a secular state where every citizen enjoys religious freedom.

questions.text: Minorities have the right to establish and administer ________.
questions.explanation: Cultural and Educational Rights protect the heritage and education of minorities.

questions.text: Which right was called the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution by Dr. Ambedkar?
questions.explanation: This right allows citizens to move the court if any other fundamental right is violated.

questions.text: Writs are:
options: Written orders issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts
questions.explanation: Courts issue writs to enforce fundamental rights.

questions.text: NHRC stands for:
questions.explanation: The NHRC is an independent body set up in 1993 to protect human rights in India.

questions.text: Human rights are:
options: Universal rights for all human beings
Rights to get money from the state
questions.explanation: Human rights are inherent to all human beings, regardless of nationality or status.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a Fundamental Right?
questions.explanation: The Right to Property was removed from the list of Fundamental Rights in 1978.

questions.text: The Right to Information (RTI) is an example of:
questions.explanation: RTI was enacted to make government more transparent and accountable.

questions.text: Which right was added by the Parliament recently to make basic education a fundamental right?
questions.explanation: Every child has a right to get free and compulsory elementary education.

questions.text: In a democracy, rights are necessary for:
options: Protecting minorities from the oppression of majority
questions.explanation: Rights act as a safeguard for all citizens and ensure a truly democratic life.

questions.text: What happens when someone's rights are violated by the government?
options: They can approach the High Court or Supreme Court
questions.explanation: The judiciary is the guardian of fundamental rights.

questions.text: Fundamental Rights are ________.
questions.explanation: Justiciable means they can be enforced by the courts.

questions.text: Traffic in human beings means:
options: Selling and buying of human beings
questions.explanation: This is a form of exploitation prohibited by the Constitution.

questions.text: Who appoints the commissioners of the NHRC?
questions.explanation: The President appoints them, usually retired judges or experts.

questions.text: South Africa's new Constitution gives citizens the right to:
questions.explanation: The South African Bill of Rights is very comprehensive.

questions.text: Fundamental rights can be suspended during ________.
questions.explanation: Under certain circumstances, the government can restrict rights for national security.

questions.text: A 'Minority' in the constitutional sense is based on:
questions.explanation: Constitutional protections apply to both religious and linguistic minorities.

questions.text: Which of these is a restriction on the Right to Freedom of Speech?
options: One cannot instigate people to violence
One cannot use it to start a war
questions.explanation: Rights are not absolute and come with reasonable restrictions.

questions.text: The Saudi Arabian government is a ________.
questions.explanation: The King has absolute power and citizens have limited rights.

questions.text: In India, even the ________ has to follow the law of the land.
questions.explanation: This is the essence of the Rule of Law.

questions.text: Secularism in India means:
options: The state is neutral in religious matters
The state promotes the majority religion
questions.explanation: The state does not privilege any one religion.

questions.text: The scope of rights is ________ over time.
questions.explanation: New rights like Right to Privacy or Right to Clean Water are being recognized by courts.

questions.text: Fundamental rights are part of which section of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part III of the Indian Constitution contains the list of Fundamental Rights.

questions.text: What is the primary function of the NHRC?
options: To conduct inquiry into human rights violations
questions.explanation: It investigates complaints and makes recommendations to the government.

questions.text: Right to Constitutional Remedies is a ________ right.
questions.explanation: It is the sixth fundamental right.

questions.text: In Kosovo, Albanians were ________ of the total population.
questions.explanation: Despite being the majority in Kosovo, they were targeted by the Serb-dominated Yugoslav government.

questions.text: The prisoners in Guantanamo Bay were not given:
questions.explanation: This was a clear violation of international laws.

questions.text: Citizens' rights in a democracy are a check on ________.
questions.explanation: Rights prevent the government from becoming tyrannical.

questions.text: Freedom of movement means citizens can ________.
options: Cross international borders without a visa
questions.explanation: Indian citizens have the right to move freely throughout the territory of India.
